title: Mullin's Kent Procession
title_candidates: Wire to Wire on the Romney Marshes
By the Length of a Putt and the Width of a 10
theme: David Mullin's wire-to-wire double — Trophy and Jacket grabbed at Royal Cinque Ports and never surrendered — with a late R4 wobble that briefly let Alex Baker dream, until Alex did what Alex did all week at the 16th.
opening_hook: Open at the 16th tee on Sunday at Prince's: Mullin's eight-point cushion has shrivelled, Alex Baker is charging, and then Alex makes a 10. Cut back to Deal on Thursday to explain how we got there.
foreshadow: Alex Baker's quintuple-bogey 10 at the 16th at Royal Cinque Ports in R1 (b04) — a postcard from the future
Mullin's R1 burst from the 9th (b16) that set the platform he never stepped off
Jon Baker's quiet slide to the bottom of the Spoon race at the 4th on day one (b31)

competitions.name: Trophy (Stableford)
competitions.how: Mullin drew level on R1 H11 (b41) and went outright on R1 H16 (b38) on the back of a 13-point burst from the 9th (b16); he then led every subsequent hole of the tournament. The lead ballooned to nine after R2 and stood at eight entering R4. A quadruple bogey at the 10th on Sunday (b08) opened the door, and Alex Baker's R4 charges (b22, b19) closed the gap to nervous distance — until Alex carded a 10 at the 16th (b02), settling the matter. Final margin: 2.

competitions.name: Green Jacket (Gross)
competitions.how: Mullin levelled with Alex Baker at R1 H9 (b40) and was alone in front by the end of round one. Gross rounds of 15, 14, 20 and 19 gave him a lead that grew to 12 by R2, held at 12 through R3, and never seriously threatened. Jon Baker — yes, Spoon and Jacket-runner-up, the maths of handicapping — finished 2nd at +7 back (b03).

competitions.name: Wooden Spoon
competitions.how: Jon dropped to last on the 4th hole of R1 (b31) and remained there for the next 68 holes. Stableford rounds of 29, 29, 28 kept him pinned; a respectable 39 in R4 was too little, too late, despite a 14-point burst from the 12th (b06). Final tally 125.

headline_candidates: Deal Me In: Mullin Plants the Flag at Royal Cinque Ports
rounds.chosen_headline: Deal Me In: Mullin Plants the Flag at Royal Cinque Ports
rounds.angle: Mullin's 40-point opener — built on the 13-point burst from the 9th (b16) — gives him outright leads in both competitions by the back nine, while Alex Baker's quintuple-bogey 10 at the 16th (b04) writes the first draft of his week.

A 41-point round stretches Mullin's Trophy cushion to nine and his Jacket lead to twelve; he twice halts mid-round wobbles with birdies (b20, b21) while Alex Baker bleeds twelve shots over four holes (b29).

headline_candidates: An 8 at the 8th, and Still in Front
rounds.angle: Mullin shrugs off a quadruple-bogey 9 at the 8th (b07) and a sticky front nine, then closes 15-18 in 13 points (b17) for the round's defining ten-point split (b18); cracks elsewhere (b13, b24, b28) hint at fragility.

headline_candidates: The Chase, and What Ended It
rounds.angle: Mullin's quadruple bogey at the 10th (b08) cuts the lead from eight to genuinely uncomfortable; Alex Baker rallies with bursts at 1-3 (b22) and 13-15 (b19) to within touching distance — and then, at the 16th, makes a 10 (b02, b26). Jon Baker's 39 (b06) is the day's quiet good news.

players.arc: Wire-to-wire double — outright Trophy leader from R1 H16 onwards and Jacket leader by close of play day one; survived a R3 quad at the 8th and a R4 quad at the 10th to win both by margins comfortable enough on paper, uncomfortable enough in the moment.

Trophy runner-up by 2, and the week's most entertaining man to follow: hot stretches at Prince's bookended by big numbers, including two 10s — one at the 16th at Deal, one at the 16th at Prince's, the latter ending the chase.
Spoon-holder from the 4th hole of R1 to the last, yet finished 2nd in the Jacket — handicapping doing exactly what it says on the tin; signed off with a 39 and a 14-point flurry in R4.
Quietly mid-pack, with a R1 quad at the 3rd (b15) and a R3 collapse at the 12th (b28) bookending some respectable golf in between (b33).

venue_notes: TEG's second visit to Kent (2021), a four-round links pilgrimage: Royal Cinque Ports at Deal (2nd TEG visit), Littlestone on the Romney Marshes (2nd TEG visit), and two days at Prince's Shore/Dunes in Sandwich (the 3rd TEG round there by Sunday). Lean on the Open-pedigree weight of Deal in the opener, the remote-marshes character of Littlestone in R2, and Prince's as the closing stage where the tournament was both nearly lost and emphatically settled — at the 16th.
